adaptation_notes: Illustrative Stage 1 only — not full film.

global_production_variables.directorial_treatment: 35mm anamorphic, high-contrast winter daylight, film grain

Character_P.description: Adult man, 28, thin wiry frame, short dark hair, pizza polo without name tag.
Character_P.voice_profile: American male late 20s, soft mid baritone, tired Midwestern, measured pace; same voice every scene.

Character_N_Young.description: Boy about 12, sturdy, reddish-brown hair, dirty t-shirt, child proportions.
Character_N_Young.voice_profile: American preteen boy, not adult baritone; confident kid energy; same voice in flashbacks.

scenes.setting: Day 1 — Kirk Street alley flashback, summer daylight
scenes.dramatic_function: Show Nick taking blame for broken window
scenes.summary: Kids play kickball; Mark smashes Mrs. Engel's window; young Nick steps up and claims fault.
scenes.wardrobe_notes: Dirty kid clothes; not adult gym bodies

story_beats.visual_event: Wide alley: kids play kickball; garbage-can bases; N_Young ready; P_Young watches.

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The thing about Nick was, he was a real-life Viking…

story_beats.visual_event: Mark kicks red kickball; ball smashes Mrs. Engel kitchen window; glass shatters.
story_beats.blocking_notes: Clear flight path into brick house window — not over a wall.

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: One time little Mark Conner kicked a ball right through old Mrs. Engel's kitchen…

must_not: ball goes over wall instead of through window

story_beats.visual_event: Mrs. Engel yells from shattered window; Nick steps toward house looking up at her.
story_beats.blocking_notes: Camera behind Nick; he faces house/window; back to camera; never faces viewer only.

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: But Nick stepped right up to her and said he was the one who did it…

must_not: Nick facing camera with house behind him
